front: Work: attacks psychologism in its first volume; Volume II contains the rallying cry of phenomenology, 'back to the things themselves'
Work: identifies three strata of logic: morphology of meanings, logic of consequence, and metalogic
Work: examines mereology (the relationship between wholes and parts), in which objects not made of indivisible atoms are called 'gunk'
Concept: suspending judgment about the existence of the natural world while analyzing one's experience with phenomena; adopted from the Greek skeptics
Concept: coined in Husserl's Ideas, this method focuses on first-person experience to identify essential features of phenomena
Concept: Husserl's distinction between conscious intentional acts and their objects-of-intention, extending Brentano's idea of intentionality
Concept: Husserl's Aristotelian-derived term for sensory material formed from the intentional content of perceptual experience
Work: five 'meditations' modeled on Descartes, based on 1929 Sorbonne lectures; Eugen Fink added a sixth; first translated into French by Levinas
Concept: the pregiven framework of beliefs and attitudes shaping shared human experience; introduced in The Crisis of European Sciences; Habermas synthesized it with 'system'
Philosopher: this thinker's first book, Philosophy of Arithmetic, was criticized for psychologism by Gottlob Frege; studied mathematics under Karl Weierstrass
Philosopher: Heidegger dedicated Being and Time to this thinker but rescinded the dedication due to this thinker's Jewish ancestry
Philosopher: Edith Stein wrote her dissertation on empathy under this thinker, later became a Carmelite nun, died at Auschwitz, and became a patron saint of Europe
Philosopher: argued 'naturalism refutes itself' in the paper 'Philosophy as Rigorous Science'
Philosopher: Derrida examined this thinker in Speech and Phenomena and wrote on the 'Origin of Geometry' essay
Philosopher: argued perception of time depends on retention and protention
Philosopher: Herman Van Breda saved this thinker's archives from the Nazis
